label: Squarish 2BR — public/private split, stacked LDK (living→dining→kitchen front-to-rear)

adjacencies.note: ensuite's south wall is standard's north wall — solid (no door, ensuite is private to master)
common's south wall is standard's north wall — solid (door faces dining across the dividing wall)
shared plumbing wall between the two baths — single wet stack on the bath bar
ensuite's north wall is master's south wall — this is the ensuite door (only access to ensuite)
common's north wall is master's south wall — solid (no door, common is publicly accessed)
standard accessed across the dividing wall from living (both at front)
master accessed across the dividing wall from dining (both toward rear)
common bath's door faces dining across the dividing wall — public access

notes: Strict public/private zone split. The envelope is partitioned by a single
vertical wall: LDK in one half, sleeping + baths in the other.
Public column (front to rear): living, dining, kitchen — open-plan stack.
Private column (front to rear): standard, [ensuite | common side by side],
master. The bath bar in the middle is also the wet core (ensuite + common
share a plumbing wall).
Cross-wall access (each across the dividing wall):
- standard <-> living (both at front)
- common  <-> dining  (bath door faces dining — common is publicly
accessible without entering the bedrooms)
- master  <-> dining  (both toward rear)
match_bedroom_widths keeps master and standard the same width so the
private column reads as a clean stack rather than a cantilever.
Consolidates two earlier patterns (hallway-based split + clustered-baths
split) into one. The hallway is no longer required because the bath bar
itself separates the two bedrooms; standard and master each have their
own cross-wall door to the public LDK.
Companion topology: squarish_two_bedroom_split_openplan.json — same
private column, but dining and kitchen sit side-by-side at the rear of
the public column rather than vertically stacked.
